name: Dragon Khan & Serengueti
description:
This loop is not for the faint of heart and will give you some adrenaline rushes, I promise! It goes northwest from Tremp to the legendary trails "Dragon Khan" and "Serengueti".

Dragon Khan is pure thrill! This terracotta-colored ridge path is the flagship and probably the most famous path of Tremp. The almost one kilometer long, extremely narrow path really packs a punch. The path does not forgive any mistakes and there are only very few places where you can interrupt the journey and continue on foot. It's best to take a close look at what's coming beforehand and then decide whether you want to walk the fine line. A tiny wobble and you're lying in the gorge below.

Then it's much easier on the nerves. Via the strenuous uphill trail "Pujada de la Muerte" you crank up to the well-known "Serengueti" trail, which resembles a fun roller coaster ride. This path is comparatively easy to ride and promises a lot of flow. Here, too, there are occasional exposed spots that require your full concentration.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tremp, located within the ORIGENS UNESCO Global Geopark in the Spanish Pyrenees, offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features an extensive network of singletrack trails, characterized by red clay soils, rocky outcrops, and dry, natural terrain. Riders can expect varied topography, including technical descents, flowy sections, and exposed cliffside paths leading to viewpoints over the Sant Antoni Reservoir. This environment provides a range of routes suitable for different skill levels, from challenging multi-hour rides to more accessible loops. Best mountain bike trails around Tremp The most popular mountain bike trail is Roques Pelades, a 14.5 miles (23.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route features extended descents over rougher terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Els Escorpins, a moderate 14.6 miles (23.5 km) path. This route is part of an extensive network of first-class singletrack trails. Local mountain bikers also love the Dragon Khan & Serengueti, a 11.6 miles (18.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with flowy sections, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Tremp is defined by red clay soils, rocky outcrops, and dry, natural trails with views of the Pyrenean landscape and Sant Antoni Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging, technical descents. The routes in Tremp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 2,800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tremp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Tremp?
A: Tremp offers a wide selection of mountain biking opportunities, with over 60 routes available on komoot. These range from easy loops to challenging, multi-hour rides, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Tremp?
A: The trails around Tremp feature a diverse landscape, including red clay soils, rocky outcrops, and dry, natural singletrack. You'll encounter technical descents, flowy sections, and exposed cliffside paths, often with stunning views of the Pyrenean landscape and the Sant Antoni Reservoir. The unique geology of areas like the Slick Rock Pallares offers excellent traction on stone.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Tremp?
A: Yes, while many trails require good to very good riding technique, Tremp does offer options for all skill levels. There are easy loops available, and specific trails like Esmolets are known to be suitable for families. For a moderate experience with extensive singletrack, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154273296/els-escorpins" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Escorpins&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tremp?
A: Tremp benefits from a Mediterranean climate, allowing for year-round mountain biking. While summers can be hot, early starts or higher-altitude routes remain enjoyable. The dry climate also often permits comfortable winter riding, making it a versatile destination for cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Tremp?
A: Yes, Tremp offers circular mountain bike routes. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944285118/zona-geobike-tremp-circular-por-el-pallars-jussa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zona Geobike — Tremp - Circular por el Pallars Jussà&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 65 km and provides a comprehensive tour of the Pallars Jussà region.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in Tremp?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Tremp has several difficult routes. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154247472/roques-pelades" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roques Pelades&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult trail known for its extended descents over rougher terrain. Other areas like Roc de Neret feature natural rock features and playful slab riding, while Roques Pelades and Upper Lines offer extended descents with rougher terrain.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Tremp?
A: Many trails offer breathtaking views of the Pyrenean peaks and the glittering Sant Antoni Reservoir. You can also discover historic paths that connect old villages. For spectacular natural sights, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/494069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3435367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge of the Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which are nearby attractions.

Q: Are there services like guided tours or e-MTB rentals available in Tremp?
A: Yes, Tremp is well-equipped for mountain bikers. You can find guided MTB tours, shuttle services to maximize descents, and e-MTB rentals, making the region accessible and enjoyable for a wide range of riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Tremp?
A: The mountain biking trails in Tremp are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. Riders often praise the extensive network of first-class singletrack, the varied terrain from flowy sections to technical descents, and the stunning views of the Pyrenees and Sant Antoni Reservoir.

Q: Are there any trails with flowy sections in Tremp?
A: Yes, Tremp's trail network includes routes with enjoyable flowy sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154269028/dragon-khan-serengueti" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dragon Khan & Serengueti&lt;/a&gt; trail, for example, is known for its varied terrain and flowy characteristics, providing a fun and engaging ride.

Q: What are some unique geological features I might encounter on the trails?
A: The region is known for its unique geology, particularly in areas like the Slick Rock Pallares trails. Here, you'll find remarkable stone surfaces where traction often exceeds expectations, offering a distinctive riding experience over natural rock features.
tips: This trail is awesome! Dragon Khan is not super difficult, but extremely narrow. A tire width goes on it, but not much more. The trail is unforgiving and offers very few stopping options. So, think twice before daring this adventure. Anyone who rides this trail has a very precise driving technique and damn strong nerves. Chapeau!

Wow, what a great uphill trail. Instead of taking the steep gravel road for the ascent, the trail builders have conjured up a beautiful path in the landscape that winds its way up and is really fun. Simply great!

Starting point of the Serengeti trail. A spectacular descent of 3.3 kilometers that will leave us speechless.

During the descent, with caution, we will be able to contemplate the Sant Antoni reservoir, which will be on our left.
